title: What's the maximum number of mls of lip filler that should be injected at once？
filename: What_s the maximum number of mls of lip filler that should be injected at once？.en.vtt
excerpt: So patients will often ask you how many mils they can add in one session [Music] it's worth thinking about what you're actually doing in terms of lip augmentation you're inflating something which does have a fixed amount of space now if a patient's lips are very small paradoxical…

segments.text: so patients will often ask you how many mils they can add in one session [Music] it's worth thinking about what you're actually doing in terms of lip augmentation you're inflating something which does have a fixed amount of space now if a patient's lips are very small paradoxically you may have a lower limit in terms of what you can put into their lips without starting to lose the shape so i think about lips a little bit like you might think about you know those animal balloons that have a specific shape you inflate them and it looks like a giraffe or it looks like a you know whatever um now lips are a bit like that as well except they're not animals but if you inflate them basically what will happen is eventually you will lose the shape so they get rounder and rounder because as you add pressure into something with an enclosed shape it will get rounder and so you will obliterate all the details that actually make lips beautiful so lower lip angle tubercles definition all of that turns into just a round inflated shapeless structure and the more you put it in one session the more likely that

will happen because filler unfortunately isn't entirely static if it was as easy as whatever you inject is what you see and it stays that way then that would be easy for us we could just put in exactly how we want it to look and make a decision on each injection but most filler will actually attract a bit of moisture so mostly you get a little bit of extra volume with time as it goes on and secondly you get inflammation at the time of injecting so that also hides some of what you're actually able to see and assess and for that reason i like to put a limit of around about one mil for most people now i never say never for any particular rule of thumb there's always an exception and you might meet a patient who's had two mills in previous treatments maybe you haven't seen them for three years you know most of the filler's gone you can tell by the pictures that they can take that kind of volume and you may make the decision to do more than one meal in one session that's entirely up to you and you can make the judgment to do more or less depending on the patient and their appetite for risk and their the way that you understand their lips i'd say 80 to people it's one meal with

an occasional 20 of people who might take two mils and of course it also depends on your product so it depends how volumizing your product is if you're using a product that's less good at volume and better definition which is often the tension you may want to use slightly more to get the same amount of volume but rule of thumb is one mil possibly two mills in 20 people hope that helps
